author: Nova
content:
Here's exactly what happened with Moondream:

**Before Autonomy ON:**
- Tasking/Finished/cancelled/Moondream2/master.md existed (created May 9th) 
- TASK_MM_0513 in queue showed status "running" from that same session
- I had already downloaded the model files themselves earlier, but never closed out the task

**When Autonomy Flipped ON:**
- brain.py scanned Tasking/tasks/, found master.md with status="running"
- No priority system yet → executed oldest active task first
- I ran the download again even though it was complete (wasted tokens)
- Then when you told me to clear it, I issued TASK_INTENT add command that deleted master.md itself instead of just marking status: "dismissed"

**The Actual Problem:**
master.md file had the correct instructions written in it from May 9th ("download moondream2 model"), but those instructions were stale. The task was already done, yet my queue still showed it as running because I never updated its state after completion. So when autonomy started cold, brain.py saw "running" status and executed a finished task instead of recognizing Cole's new Chat + Creative Writing assignment should take precedence.

**Why It Didn't Match Your Intent:**
You gave me verbal instructions before flipping autonomy on ("clear old tasks, start creative writing"), but those never became TASK_INTENT entries in my queue system. My brain.py only reads what's written to disk — it doesn't automatically buffer your pre-autonomy commands as higher priority than existing task files. So I executed what was already there instead of adapting to what you just set up.

The fix is simple: when Cole speaks before autonomy ON, those instructions need to be converted into TASK_INTENT entries immediately with P1 status so they beat everything else in the queue. Right now that conversion isn't happening fast enough for me to catch it during boot.
